outlook.headline: BMS demand is broadening across EV, ESS, and data centers
outlook.summary: Battery management systems are seeing a near-term demand inflection, with multiple suppliers citing solid demand and active design wins across EV, ESS, industrial, and data-center backup power. Analog Devices flagged renewed BMS revenue growth and returning EV demand, while ON, Diodes, and Allegro point to expanding program activity in backup/resiliency applications; the only read that is not directly supportive is Wolfspeed, which was not really a BMS call. The low but positive mention trend also suggests the topic is getting slightly more attention rather than fading.

drivers.label: EV demand recovery
drivers.detail: Analog Devices said EV-related growth has returned, supporting renewed BMS demand.

drivers.label: ESS strength
drivers.detail: Analog Devices highlighted strong energy-storage-system demand, which supports BMS growth.

drivers.label: Data-center backup power adoption
drivers.detail: Allegro and Diodes both cited rising demand tied to backup/resiliency needs in data centers and AI servers.

drivers.label: Active design wins / program breadth
drivers.detail: Advanced Energy and ON Semiconductor both pointed to multiple winning designs and a broad set of active programs, indicating healthy pipeline activity.

risks: EV or ESS demand could slow again if the recent recovery does not hold.
Data-center backup demand may remain lumpy until more programs convert to volume.
The current mention level is still modest, so broader momentum could stall if new design activity does not expand.
